steps.title: Turn the slope into a second point
steps.content: The line has slope $-\frac{1}{2}$ and passes through $(6,4)$. For a quick Desmos method, move 2 units right and 1 unit down to get another point, $(8,3)$. Fit $(6,4)$ and $(8,3)$ with a linear regression.

steps.title: Solve for the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content: The regression gives slope $-\frac{1}{2}$ and intercept $7$, so the equation is $$y=-\frac{x}{2}+7.$$ That matches choice D, so D is correct.
